key_insight: A geometry-conditioned Fourier neural operator can learn the one-step dynamics of the cubic NLS on periodic domains and distinguish the different high-frequency behaviors induced by rational versus irrational torus aspect ratios.
engineering_relevance: This suggests neural operators can serve as fast surrogate solvers for dispersive PDEs while explicitly incorporating geometric parameters that affect spectral transfer and long-time stability.
method: Train a Fourier neural operator on real/imaginary solution fields plus the aspect-ratio parameter ω²
Predict the next time-step of the cubic defocusing NLS on 2D flat tori
Run ablations on Fourier modes, activation choice, depth, and geometry conditioning
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on unseen trajectories generated by random-phase initial data with a Fourier pseudospectral solver, and it reproduced the contrasting H² growth trends on rational and irrational tori.
watch_item: Long-time accuracy depends on explicit geometry conditioning and may degrade if the Fourier truncation or training distribution fails to capture resonance-sensitive spectral transfer.
paper_title: Operator Learning for Cubic Nonlinear Schrödinger Equation on Periodic Domains
